affect_table_type.brief: A structure representing an entry in the affect table, encapsulating its name, category, and modifiability status.
affect_table_type.details: The affect_table_type struct is a core data structure used to define and manage entries within the game's affect system. It contains a String member 'name' that uniquely identifies the affect, an 'type' member of affect::type that categorizes the affect (such as spell effects, weapon attributes, or other effect types), and a boolean 'settable' indicating whether the affect entry can be modified during runtime. This struct enables efficient lookup, categorization, and configuration of affects, supporting dynamic affect management, system customization, and organized effect processing. It is commonly used in collections and functions like affect_index_lookup to identify, compare, and manipulate affect entries based on their attributes, facilitating flexible and maintainable affect handling within the game architecture.
affect_table_type.notes: This struct is designed to be lightweight and easily integrated into collections such as vectors or maps for efficient searching and management. Its use of a custom String class allows for flexible string operations, including case-insensitive comparisons, which can be beneficial for user-facing features.
affect_table_type.rationale: affect_table_type exists to provide a standardized way to represent and manage different effects within the game system. By encapsulating key attributes—name, type, and mutability—it supports organized effect categorization, dynamic configuration, and consistent referencing, thereby enhancing the modularity and maintainability of the affect management subsystem.
affect_table_type.name: affect_table_type

usages.tables_8cc, int affect_index_lookup(cons: Provides the 'name' field used in comparison operations within the loop to identify matching entries.

name.brief: Stores the identifier or label for an affect table entry.
name.details: The 'name' variable holds the label or identifier associated with an affect table entry, utilizing the custom String class that offers enhanced string functionalities such as case-insensitive comparisons and advanced manipulation methods. It is initialized upon creation of an affect table entry and is used for referencing, display, and lookup operations within the affect management system. Its design leverages the extended features of the String class to support flexible and robust string handling in various contexts.
name.notes: The use of the custom String class allows for case-insensitive operations and more sophisticated string manipulations, which are beneficial for matching and displaying affect names consistently across different parts of the system.
name.rationale: This variable exists to uniquely identify affect entries by name, enabling efficient referencing and user-friendly display, while supporting flexible string operations necessary for the system's functionality.
name.name: name

type.brief: The 'type' variable indicates the category of an affect, such as spell effects or weapon attributes.
type.details: This variable stores a value from the 'affect::type' enumeration, which defines various categories of effects, attributes, or identifiers used within the game system. It is initialized when an affect instance is created and is essential for determining how the affect interacts with game entities, enabling the application of specific behaviors or effects based on its category. The 'type' field helps organize affect instances, ensuring consistent handling and processing of different effect types throughout the game logic.
type.notes: The 'type' variable is typically stored as an enum value, facilitating efficient comparisons and effect categorization. Its consistent use across affect instances supports standardized effect management.
type.rationale: This variable exists to categorize affects, allowing the game system to apply appropriate behaviors and effects based on their type, thereby supporting organized and predictable effect processing.
type.name: type

settable.brief: Flag indicating whether the affect table can be modified at runtime.
settable.details: The `settable` boolean variable determines if the affect table is currently configurable, allowing modifications during runtime. It is initialized based on application configuration or user permissions and may be updated dynamically to enable or restrict editing capabilities. This variable works in conjunction with control logic to enforce access restrictions and maintain data consistency within the affect management system.
settable.notes: Typically set during initialization and updated in response to permission changes or application state transitions.
settable.rationale: This variable exists to control the mutability of the affect table, supporting dynamic configuration and ensuring that modifications occur only when appropriate, thereby safeguarding data integrity.
settable.name: settable
